title: sapium insigne var. genuinum
intro: Sapium insigne var. genuinum, also known as the Yellow Myrtle or Yellow Bitter Ash, is a plant species in the family Euphorbiaceae. It is traditionally used in medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The plant has various health benefits, such as treating fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as being used as a natural remedy for digestive issues. Herbal preparations of Sapium insigne var. genuinum include infusions, decoctions, and ointments, which can be made by steeping the leaves, bark, or roots in water or oil.
